name: The Clash
bio: The Clash has a catalog with durable streaming, playlist, publishing, and licensing value that makes it a useful future addition to How Much Music.
notable_for: The Clash is best known here for signature songs that remain recognizable enough to support long-tail catalog demand.
career_highlight: Songs like London Calling and Should I Stay or Should I Go can anchor the artist page with clear catalog economics and internal links.
revenue_drivers: Catalog streaming and playlist use keep the core songs active.
Live-culture recognition and sync use can renew demand.
Long-tail fan discovery supports recurring earnings beyond release cycles.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps The Clash's headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: The Clash's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
